answer:
The Front Core API uses Bearer token authentication for all requests [1]. You must include the token in the Authorization header of every request, formatted as follows:

```curl
curl --request GET \
  --url https://api2.frontapp.com/conversations/cnv_123/drafts \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer [YOUR_TOKEN]'
```

You can obtain a token either by generating an API token directly within your Front account settings for personal or private use, or by implementing OAuth 2.0 [1][2]. OAuth is required for all public integrations available to other Front customers [1][2]. When using OAuth, you initially use Basic authentication—with your Base64-encoded Client ID and Client Secret—to exchange a temporary authorization code for your API and refresh tokens [3]. Subsequent API requests then revert to using the Bearer token method [3].
